mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by more than 0.5 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.5pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a win rate above 52% in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days exclusively during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 20-day returns in 60% or more of observations during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic activity and wage growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for PAYEMS changes remains below 0.01 across all 5-day forward return windows in a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth surpassing 3.0 percent annually forces monetary authorities to tighten policy expectations, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in over 65% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds the 3.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms synchronized demand expansion across production and consumption channels, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with a classification accuracy below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production signals contracting manufacturing capacity and weak business investment, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate above 55% during late-cycle expansion periods.
